name: Symbol Duplication Detector
short_description: Detects exact code clones (Type-1/Type-2) via jscpd token-based analysis and near-duplicate semantic clones (Type-3/Type-4) via embedding-based similarity across Kotlin and Swift modules, targeting duplicated formatters, converters, and validators in a multi-Kit crypto codebase.
tech_stack: jscpd (v3.x, kucherenko, 150+ languages including Kotlin and Swift) — Rabin-Karp token-based exact/near-exact clone detection, generates JSON reports
UniXcoder or CodeBERT (microsoft, huggingface) — semantic embedding for Type-3/4 clone detection across cross-language pairs (Kotlin ↔ Swift)
semhash (MinishLab, v0.x) — fast multimodal semantic deduplication using MinHash + embedding, useful for function-level near-dedup
tree-sitter (kotlin + swift grammars) — function/class boundary extraction to feed per-symbol embeddings
custom Python orchestrator — splits files into symbol windows, runs embedding, computes cosine similarity, thresholds clone pairs
entity_model: Produces :CloneGroup nodes (id, type=[exact|near-exact|semantic], clone_count, representative_file, representative_symbol) and :IS_CLONE_OF edges (from_symbol, to_symbol, similarity_score, clone_type). :Symbol nodes (name, kind=[function|class|object], file_path, line_start, line_end, language) are produced as intermediate entities — shared with Item 21 Symbol Index Extractor. Applies structural facet structural=duplication-cluster. For crypto-domain near-duplicates, domain-concept=formatting|conversion|validation facets are attached when function names match heuristic patterns (format*, convert*, validate*, parse*).
storage_format: graph-node — :CloneGroup and :IS_CLONE_OF edges written to Graphiti. Rationale: clone groups need graph traversal to identify refactoring scope. A supplementary .knowledge/clone-summary.md is written with top-20 clone groups for human review.

prior_art.name: jscpd
prior_art.description: Copy/paste detector supporting 150+ languages including Kotlin and Swift. Uses Rabin-Karp algorithm. v3.x actively maintained. MIT license. Supports strict/mild/weak symbol-skip modes. MegaLinter integrates it. AI-reporter mode added for agent-driven refactoring. Last release: 2024.

prior_art.name: semhash
prior_art.description: Fast multimodal semantic deduplication using MinHash + embeddings. Language-agnostic. Used for dataset dedup, adaptable to code.

prior_art.name: UniXcoder
prior_art.description: Cross-modal pre-trained model for programming languages. Achieves mean F1=0.918 on code clone benchmarks, lowest variance. Supports Java, C++, Python, Go, Ruby, PHP — Kotlin and Swift require fine-tuning or approximation via Java/C++ proxy. Apache 2.0.
prior_art.maintenance: active research baseline; no active 2025 releases but widely used

prior_art.name: CodeBERT
prior_art.description: Microsoft pre-trained model for code. Multi-lingual (Python, Java, JS, PHP, Ruby, Go). Useful for near-miss clone detection. Does not natively support Kotlin or Swift.

prior_art.name: LeONet / visual clone analysis
prior_art.description: 2025 paper: visual clone analysis method using AST feature extraction hybrid approach. Provides F1 benchmarks on BigCloneBench.

prior_art.name: SmellyCode++ (Scientific Data 2025)
prior_art.description: Multi-label code smell dataset with 94-96% F1 for structural detection including duplicate-code smells. Cross-reference for dataset benchmarking.

prior_art.name: Aider repo-map (tree-sitter based)
prior_art.description: Tree-sitter-backed repository map for LLM context. Symbol extraction approach is reusable for windowing code into per-function segments for embedding.

consumer_query_patterns:
// Find all exact clone groups with 3+ members
MATCH (g:CloneGroup {type: 'exact'})
WHERE g.clone_count >= 3
RETURN g.id, g.representative_symbol, g.clone_count ORDER BY g.clone_count DESC

// Which modules contain near-duplicate amount formatting functions?
MATCH (s:Symbol)-[:IS_CLONE_OF]->(t:Symbol)
WHERE s.name CONTAINS 'format' AND s.language = 'kotlin'
RETURN s.file_path, t.file_path, s.name, t.name

// All semantic duplicates between Kotlin and Swift for a given module pair
MATCH (s:Symbol {language: 'kotlin'})-[c:IS_CLONE_OF {clone_type: 'semantic'}]->(t:Symbol {language: 'swift'})
WHERE s.file_path CONTAINS $module_a OR t.file_path CONTAINS $module_b
RETURN s.name, t.name, c.similarity_score ORDER BY c.similarity_score DESC
graphiti_episode_type: EntityNode — :CloneGroup nodes are structural findings that persist until refactored (carry end_time when the duplicate is removed); :IS_CLONE_OF edges are semi-permanent with end_time on resolution.
ingest_perf_est: [uncertain] jscpd exact-clone pass: ~2–5 minutes full scan on 350K LOC (Kotlin+Swift). Semantic embedding pass (per-function): highly variable — without GPU, ~20–60 minutes for 20 libraries at function granularity using UniXcoder; with GPU acceleration, ~3–10 minutes. Incremental (changed files only): jscpd ~30 seconds; embedding re-embedding of touched functions ~1–3 minutes. Estimates based on analogy with similar embedding pipeline benchmarks, not direct measurement.
failure_modes: jscpd has no native semantic understanding — renamed variable/method clones (Type-3) may be missed at low token-similarity thresholds; high threshold causes false negatives on legitimate refactors
UniXcoder/CodeBERT not natively trained on Kotlin or Swift — semantic embedding quality degrades vs Java/Python baselines; cross-language (Kotlin↔Swift) clone detection particularly uncertain
Generated code (SKIE-generated Swift wrappers, KSP-generated Kotlin code) creates high-volume exact clones that are not meaningful refactoring targets — noise filtering required
Large functions (>500 tokens) may exceed embedding model context windows causing truncation and missed similarities
jscpd minimum-tokens threshold tuning is per-project; too low = noise, too high = misses short but important duplicates (e.g., 5-line address validation logic)
open_questions: What is the right granularity for semantic clone detection — file-level, function-level, or class-level? Function-level is most actionable but 10x more expensive than file-level.
How to filter SKIE/KSP generated code from clone analysis? Generated files typically live in build/ directories but may be copied to source directories in some KMP setups.

Is cross-language (Kotlin ↔ Swift) semantic clone detection a P1 requirement for Unstoppable Wallet? If KMP shared code is the single source of truth, cross-language duplicates are expected and valid — this changes the extractor's role fundamentally.
Overlap with Item 21 (Symbol Index): should :Symbol nodes be co-owned or replicated? Replication creates inconsistency risk; co-ownership requires guaranteed ingest ordering.
Embedding model choice: UniXcoder (Python, HuggingFace) vs a code-aware LLM API (e.g. Claude with code embedding) — determinism vs cost tradeoff unresolved.
adversarial_resistance: medium — jscpd token-based detection is robust to simple renaming but defeated by structural refactoring; embedding-based detection is robust to renaming but sensitive to model quality for Kotlin/Swift.
